project.name: mbgc
project.description: Personal board game collection app with BoardGameGeek integration. Track games, organize vibes, and sync your collection from BGG.

nodes.name: embed.go
nodes.summary: Embeds the migrations directory into the Go binary using go:embed, making SQL migration files available at runtime without filesystem access.

nodes.name: store_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the game store's URL validation logic, specifically covering valid and invalid rules URL patterns via table-driven tests.

nodes.name: TestValidateRulesURL
nodes.summary: Table-driven test that exercises validateRulesURL with various URL inputs to confirm correct acceptance and rejection behavior.

nodes.name: bgg.go
nodes.summary: BoardGameGeek API client with throttled and authenticated HTTP transports; fetches user collections and game metadata via BGG's XML API, with 429 retry-after handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses custom http.RoundTripper chain: bggAuthTransport (token/cookie injection) wraps throttledTransport (rate-limit retry via channel tick). XML unmarshaling used for BGG's thing API; gobgg library used for collection API.

nodes.name: BGGGame
nodes.summary: Data transfer struct representing a board game fetched from BGG, holding all enriched metadata including ratings, player counts, mechanics, and language dependence.

nodes.name: Client
nodes.summary: BGG HTTP client struct wrapping authenticated and throttled transports along with the gobgg library client; exposes FetchCollection and FetchGames methods.

nodes.name: NewClient
nodes.summary: Constructs a BGG Client from optional token and cookie credentials; returns nil when neither is provided. Initializes throttled transport and gobgg client.

nodes.name: FetchCollection
nodes.summary: Fetches a user's BGG board game collection by username, returning a slice of BGG IDs using the gobgg library.

nodes.name: FetchGames
nodes.summary: Batches BGG IDs into chunks of 20 and calls fetchThingsParsed to retrieve enriched game metadata for each batch.

nodes.name: fetchThingsParsed
nodes.summary: Makes a raw HTTP request to the BGG Things XML API, unmarshals the response, and retries on empty results with context-aware cancellation.

nodes.name: bgg_extra_test.go
nodes.summary: Additional integration and unit tests for the importer package: covers bggGameToGameData mapping, no-op sync without BGG username, cookie stripping, live server fetch, and ImportBGGIDs happy path.

nodes.name: bgg_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for bgg.go's XML parsing helpers, transport layer behavior (auth token injection, cookie fallback, throttled 429 retry), and fetchThingsParsed retry-on-empty logic.

nodes.name: handler.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handler for the importer domain exposing BGG sync, CSV preview, and CSV import endpoints with per-user and admin rate limit enforcement.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: HTTP handler struct for the importer package; holds a reference to the importer Service and user/admin rate limiters for the Sync endpoint.

nodes.name: RegisterRoutes
nodes.summary: Registers the three importer HTTP routes (POST sync, POST csv/preview, POST csv/import) on the provided mux with JWT authentication middleware.

nodes.name: Sync
nodes.summary: HTTP handler for BGG sync: extracts user identity and admin flag from context, delegates to Service.Sync, and returns import results as JSON envelope.

nodes.name: CSVPreview
nodes.summary: HTTP handler that accepts a multipart CSV upload, delegates to Service.ParseCSVPreview to extract BGG IDs, and returns a list envelope.

nodes.name: CSVImport
nodes.summary: HTTP handler for importing a list of BGG IDs submitted as JSON body; validates auth and non-empty IDs, then calls Service.ImportBGGIDs.

nodes.name: handler_test.go
nodes.summary: Extensive handler-level tests for the importer HTTP endpoints covering authentication, rate limiting, full-refresh admin gating, CSV preview edge cases, CSV import deduplication, and structured log event emission.

nodes.name: model.go
nodes.summary: Domain model definitions for the importer package: SyncResult (imported/skipped/failed counts), CSVPreviewRow (BGG ID + name), and RateLimit (per-user daily sync tracking).

nodes.name: SyncResult
nodes.summary: Result struct returned by a BGG sync operation indicating how many games were imported, skipped, or failed.

nodes.name: RateLimit
nodes.summary: Struct representing a user's daily sync rate-limit state with count and reset date for quota enforcement.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Core importer business logic: orchestrates BGG collection sync with rate limiting and structured log event emission, CSV parsing, and direct BGG ID import via the game service.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Importer service struct composing a store, BGG client, game service, and profile service to execute sync and import operations.

nodes.name: Sync
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a full BGG sync: checks BGG availability, enforces rate limits, fetches the user's collection, upserts new/changed games, records the sync event, and emits structured log events (sync_start, sync_ok, sync_error).

nodes.name: ParseCSVPreview
nodes.summary: Parses a BGG export CSV (supporting both 'objectid' and 'bgg_id' column name variants), extracts valid BGG IDs and game names, skipping invalid rows.

nodes.name: ImportBGGIDs
nodes.summary: Fetches game details for a list of BGG IDs, deduplicates against existing user games, and upserts new games via the game service.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed importer store implementing per-user daily sync rate limiting (quota check, record, log) with injectable canSyncRow for testability.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Importer store struct backed by a pgx DB connection and an injectable canSyncRow function enabling unit testing without a live database.

nodes.name: CheckRateLimit
nodes.summary: Enforces per-user or per-admin daily sync quota by calling CanSync with the appropriate limit and returning apierr sentinels on rate-limit breach.

nodes.name: CanSync
nodes.summary: Queries the importer.rate_limits table and returns whether the user is within their daily sync quota, treating no prior record as allowed.

nodes.name: store_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the importer Store's CanSync rate-limit logic using an injectable canSyncRow stub: covers first sync, DB error, daily quota exhaustion, and midnight reset.

nodes.name: smoke_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration smoke tests that spin up a real pgxpool connection and an in-process HTTP mux to verify multi-tenancy game row isolation, rules-URL host validation, collections CRUD round-trip, and atomic set-game-collections behavior on partial failure.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a testHarness struct with a real pgxpool and a local HMAC JWT verifier to simulate the full request pipeline without a running server.

nodes.name: signTestToken
nodes.summary: Mints a signed HS256 JWT with custom sub/email/username/admin claims for use in smoke-test HTTP requests.

nodes.name: newHarness
nodes.summary: Initializes the test harness by opening a pgxpool connection from the TEST_DATABASE_URL env var and setting up schema fixtures; skips if env is unset.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_MultiTenancy_IsolatesGameRows
nodes.summary: Verifies that two distinct user tokens cannot see each other's game rows and that a cross-user delete is rejected.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_CollectionsCRUD_RoundTrip
nodes.summary: Exercises the full create-read-update-delete lifecycle for vibes/collections through the HTTP API and asserts correct status codes and body content.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_SetGameCollections_AtomicOnPartialFailure
nodes.summary: Asserts that setting game-to-collection memberships rolls back completely when a cross-tenant collection ID is included, verifying transactional atomicity.

nodes.name: verifier.go
nodes.summary: Implements JWT verification for Supabase-issued tokens using JWKS auto-refresh via keyfunc; exposes a RequireAuth middleware that validates Bearer tokens and injects user identity into the request context.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both ES256/RS256 via JWKS and optional HS256 via SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET; falls back gracefully when secret is empty.

nodes.name: Verifier
nodes.summary: Holds the configured keyfunc and expected issuer string; provides parse and RequireAuth methods for JWT validation.

nodes.name: NewVerifier
nodes.summary: Constructs a Verifier by fetching the JWKS endpoint from supabaseURL and optionally chaining an HS256 fallback keyfunc when jwtSecret is provided.

nodes.name: RequireAuth
nodes.summary: HTTP middleware that extracts and validates the Bearer token, rejects unauthenticated requests with 401, and populates the context with user ID, username, and admin flag via httpx.SetGatewayUser.

nodes.name: verifier_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the JWT Verifier covering missing header, malformed header, invalid/expired tokens, wrong issuer/audience, valid token context propagation, admin flag extraction, claims resolution, and envelope error response shape.

nodes.name: handler.go
nodes.summary: Provides a fail-open slog handler that routes log records to a primary handler and, on primary failure, emits a warning to a secondary meta handler to ensure observability never blocks the application.
nodes.languageNotes: failOpenHandler implements slog.Handler; Heartbeat runs a goroutine that emits periodic liveness records via httpx.Record until context cancellation.

nodes.name: failOpenHandler
nodes.summary: slog.Handler implementation that delegates to a primary handler and falls back to a meta handler on error, preventing logging failures from surfacing to callers.

nodes.name: NewHandler
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a failOpenHandler writing JSON to stdout for primary logs and stderr for meta/error logs.

nodes.name: Heartbeat
nodes.summary: Starts a ticker goroutine that emits a heartbeat log record at the given interval until the context is cancelled, used for liveness monitoring.

nodes.name: handler_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the failOpenHandler and Heartbeat function, verifying fail-open behavior on primary/meta handler errors, absence of meta output on success, and heartbeat tick emission and cancellation.

nodes.name: handler.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handler for user profile endpoints, exposing GET /api/v1/profile and PATCH /api/v1/profile/bgg-username with JWT-authenticated routes that delegate to the profile Service.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: Profile HTTP handler struct holding a reference to the profile Service; provides GetProfile and SetBGGUsername endpoint methods.

nodes.name: NewHandler
nodes.summary: Constructs a profile Handler wrapping the given Service.

nodes.name: RegisterRoutes
nodes.summary: Registers authenticated profile routes (GET profile, PATCH BGG username) on the provided HTTP mux using the auth middleware.

nodes.name: GetProfile
nodes.summary: Handles GET profile requests by extracting user ID from context and returning the profile envelope, or writing an error response on failure.

nodes.name: SetBGGUsername
nodes.summary: Handles PATCH BGG username requests, decodes and truncates the JSON body, delegates to Service.SetBGGUsername, then returns the updated profile.

nodes.name: handler_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the profile Handler using a mockProfileStore, covering authenticated and unauthenticated GetProfile, lazy upsert behavior, store errors, and SetBGGUsername success/failure scenarios.

nodes.name: model.go
nodes.summary: Defines the Profile data model struct with fields for user ID, username, BGG username, admin flag, and audit timestamps.

nodes.name: Profile
nodes.summary: Core profile entity struct with ID, Username, BGGUsername, IsAdmin, CreatedAt, and UpdatedAt fields.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Profile service layer implementing GetProfile (with lazy upsert on not-found), SetBGGUsername, and GetBGGUsername business logic on top of the profileStore interface.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Profile service struct wrapping a profileStore interface; provides GetProfile, SetBGGUsername, and GetBGGUsername operations with lazy profile creation.

nodes.name: NewService
nodes.summary: Constructs a profile Service wrapping the given profileStore.

nodes.name: GetProfile
nodes.summary: Fetches the profile for the given userID; on ErrNotFound, auto-upserts the profile record (lazy creation) before returning.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL data access layer for user profiles, implementing GetProfile, UpsertProfile, SetBGGUsername, and GetBGGUsername against the profile.users table.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Postgres store for profile.users table; wraps a pgxpool.Pool and implements GetProfile, UpsertProfile, SetBGGUsername, and GetBGGUsername with apierr sentinel mapping.

nodes.name: NewStore
nodes.summary: Constructs a Store wrapping the provided pgxpool.Pool.

nodes.name: GetProfile
nodes.summary: Queries profile.users by ID and scans into a Profile struct; maps pgx.ErrNoRows to apierr.ErrNotFound.

nodes.name: UpsertProfile
nodes.summary: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE to ensure a profile row exists, returning the upserted Profile.

nodes.name: SetBGGUsername
nodes.summary: Updates the bgg_username column for the given user; returns apierr.ErrNotFound if no row was affected.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Application entry point that bootstraps the Go API server: runs database migrations, connects to pgxpool, initializes JWT verification, seeds the admin user, wires all domain handlers, applies middleware chain (CORS, rate limiting, body limits), and handles graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Go 1.22 net/http ServeMux with pattern-based routing (method+path). Graceful shutdown via signal.Notify + context.WithTimeout.

nodes.name: runMigrations
nodes.summary: Opens a raw sql.DB connection and drives golang-migrate's Up() to apply embedded SQL migrations at server startup, with dirty-state recovery via Force(0)+Up().

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Wires the complete server: configures structured logging, loads config, runs migrations, opens pgxpool, creates JWT verifier, seeds admin user, builds all domain stores/services/handlers, registers routes with auth+rate-limit middleware, and starts HTTP server with graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: handler.go
nodes.summary: Auth HTTP handler implementing login (email or username), token refresh, logout, change-password, and auth-ping endpoints by proxying requests to the Supabase Auth REST API with per-request HTTP calls.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: Auth HTTP handler struct wrapping a userStore and a supabaseAuthClient; registers all auth routes and dispatches login/refresh/logout/changePassword/ping.

nodes.name: NewHandler
nodes.summary: Constructs an auth Handler with a userStore and a configured supabaseAuthClient backed by the given HTTP client.

nodes.name: RegisterRoutes
nodes.summary: Registers the five auth routes on the mux, applying rate limiting to login/refresh/logout and JWT auth middleware to changePassword and ping.

nodes.name: handler_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-free unit tests for the auth handler covering login (valid, invalid body, missing fields, Supabase failure, unreachable, username lookup), refresh (missing token, failure, success), logout (always-succeeds, no token), ping, and change-password (all validation and happy-path cases).

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Auth Store backed by pgxpool; provides EmailByUsername (joins auth.users + profile.users on lowercased username) and EmailByUserID for the auth handler's login and change-password flows.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed auth store implementing the userStore interface with email lookup by username and by user ID.

nodes.name: handler.go
nodes.summary: Game HTTP handler exposing CRUD for games and vibe-collections, plus discover-by-collection and rules-URL update; all endpoints require JWT auth and extract user identity via httpx helpers.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: Game HTTP handler struct that delegates all business logic to a gameService interface, covering games and collections CRUD plus discover.

nodes.name: NewHandler
nodes.summary: Constructs a game Handler wrapping the given service interface.

nodes.name: RegisterRoutes
nodes.summary: Registers all game and collection routes on the mux under /api/v1/games/* and /api/v1/collections/*, all protected by the auth middleware.

nodes.name: ListGames
nodes.summary: Handles GET /games; extracts user ID, parses pagination and search/category query params (truncating to 255 chars), calls service.ListGames, and returns a JSON envelope list.

nodes.name: Discover
nodes.summary: Handles GET /games/discover; parses collection_id and multiple filter query params (type, category, mechanic, etc.), delegates to service.Discover, and returns a JSON envelope with the discovered games and matching collection info.

nodes.name: handler_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for the game handler using a mockGameStore with injectable fn fields; covers list/get/delete game, set-game-collections, list/create/update/delete collections, and pagination clamping — all with auth and unauth variants.

nodes.name: model.go
nodes.summary: Defines core domain structs for the game package: Game (full BGG-enriched game record), GameFilter (search/pagination), Collection (vibe collection), and PlayerAid (reference card file attachment).

nodes.name: Game
nodes.summary: Primary game domain model carrying full BGG metadata (players, playtime, categories, mechanics, weight, rating) plus user-specific fields (rulesURL, timestamps).

nodes.name: Collection
nodes.summary: Domain model for a user's named game collection (vibe) with game count denormalized for list display.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Thin game Service layer that defines the gameStore interface (enabling mock injection in tests) and delegates all domain operations directly to the store; acts as the testability boundary between handler and data layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Go interface-based dependency injection: Service holds a gameStore interface, not the concrete *Store, so handler tests can inject mockGameStore without a real DB.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Game service struct that wraps the gameStore interface and exposes all game and collection operations to the HTTP handler layer.

nodes.name: service_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for game Service verifying delegation and error propagation: CreateGame, GameExistsByBGGID, UpsertBGGGame, Discover, and SetGameCollections not-found propagation.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Postgres store implementing all game and collection data access: paginated list with squirrel query builder, BGG game upsert with ON CONFLICT, transactional SetGameCollections (verifies game and collection ownership), multi-filter Discover query, and URL-validated UpdateRulesURL.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses squirrel (sq) query builder for dynamic WHERE predicates in ListGames and Discover; SetGameCollections uses an explicit transaction to enforce ownership of both game and collection IDs.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed game store implementing the gameStore interface with full CRUD for games and collections, BGG upsert, discover, and transactional collection assignment.

nodes.name: ListGames
nodes.summary: Paginated game list query using squirrel; executes a COUNT(*) for total, then the data query with OFFSET/LIMIT; supports search (full-text ilike) and category filter.

nodes.name: UpsertBGGGame
nodes.summary: Inserts or updates a BGG-enriched game row using ON CONFLICT (user_id, bgg_id) DO UPDATE; returns the game ID and a boolean indicating whether it was newly inserted.

nodes.name: SetGameCollections
nodes.summary: Transactionally replaces all collection assignments for a game: verifies game ownership, optionally validates collection IDs belong to the user, deletes existing assignments, and bulk-inserts new ones.

nodes.name: Discover
nodes.summary: Dynamic discovery query filtering games within a collection by type, category, mechanic, player count, playtime, weight, rating, language dependence, and recommended players using squirrel predicates.

nodes.name: useProfile.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook encapsulating TanStack Query data fetching and mutations for the user profile, including BGG username update and password change operations.

nodes.name: useProfile
nodes.summary: Returns profile query data plus setBGGUsername and changePassword mutations, with cache invalidation on BGG username success.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized HTTP client for all API calls, implementing JWT bearer auth, automatic token refresh with concurrent-request coalescing, multipart file upload, and typed methods for every backend endpoint (auth, games, collections, importer, profile).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a single-flight refresh pattern (refreshPromise singleton) to coalesce concurrent 401 retries into one token refresh request.

nodes.name: ApiError
nodes.summary: Custom Error subclass that carries an HTTP status code alongside the message, thrown by the request and upload helpers on non-2xx responses.

nodes.name: ensureRefreshPromise
nodes.summary: Creates or reuses an in-flight token refresh Promise, coalescing concurrent 401 retries into a single POST /auth/refresh call and clearing tokens on failure.

nodes.name: request
nodes.summary: Core typed fetch wrapper that attaches Authorization header, handles 401 auto-refresh retry, 204 no-content, and throws ApiError on non-ok responses.

nodes.name: upload
nodes.summary: Multipart FormData upload variant of request; omits Content-Type to let the browser set the boundary, with the same 401 auto-refresh retry logic.

nodes.name: mapGame
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw API game object into a typed Game domain model, splitting CSV fields into arrays and coercing numeric types.

nodes.name: queryClient.ts
nodes.summary: Configures and exports the singleton TanStack Query client with 30-second stale time, 1 retry, and no refetch-on-focus.

nodes.name: queryKeys.ts
nodes.summary: Defines typed TanStack Query key factory object covering games, game detail, collections, profile, and discover endpoints.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that mounts React in StrictMode with QueryClientProvider and HashRouter wrapping the root App component.

nodes.name: GameDetailPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-page view for a single board game, displaying metadata, description, player aids, rules URL editor, vibe/collection assignment, and a delete confirmation dialog.

nodes.name: GameDetailPage
nodes.summary: React page component that loads a game by route param id, renders all detail sections, and handles vibe toggle, player-aid upload/delete, rules URL editing, and game deletion.

nodes.name: ImportCsvPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Multi-step CSV import page (upload → preview → done) that previews rows from a user-supplied file and executes a batch import of new games not already owned.

nodes.name: ImportCsvPage
nodes.summary: React component implementing a three-step CSV import wizard with file selection, server-side preview with already-owned deduplication, and import result summary.

nodes.name: ImportPage.tsx
nodes.summary: BGG sync import page that triggers a BoardGameGeek collection sync (with optional full-refresh toggle) and displays imported/skipped/failed counts.

nodes.name: ImportPage
nodes.summary: React page component for BGG sync; requires a configured BGG username (via useProfile) and posts syncBGG with an optional full-refresh flag.

nodes.name: LoginPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Login page component with email/password form that calls the auth context login mutation and redirects to the collection on success.

nodes.name: LoginPage
nodes.summary: React page component rendering a login form that delegates to useAuth.login, handles error display, and navigates to / on success.

nodes.name: ProfilePage.tsx
nodes.summary: User profile settings page allowing the user to view/update their BGG username and change their account password, with inline success/error feedback.

nodes.name: ProfilePage
nodes.summary: React page component using useProfile mutations for BGG username save and password change, displaying per-section save buttons and status messages.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that wraps the app in AuthProvider and defines all client-side routes, including a RequireAuth guard that redirects unauthenticated users to /login.

nodes.name: RequireAuth
nodes.summary: Route guard component that checks auth state via useAuth and redirects to /login if no user is present; renders a loading spinner while auth state resolves.

nodes.name: AppRoutes
nodes.summary: Defines all application routes, nesting authenticated pages under a RequireAuth+Layout wrapper and exposing /login as a public route.

nodes.name: Layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Shell layout component providing the sticky tab bar, header with logout action, and an Outlet for nested routes; redirects unauthenticated users to /login on mount.

nodes.name: Layout
nodes.summary: Top-level layout wrapper that renders the header (with logout), tab bar, and react-router Outlet; handles auth-failure redirect and page title updates based on current pathname.

nodes.name: PlayerAidManager.tsx
nodes.summary: Self-contained component for managing player aid images on a game detail page, supporting upload, delete, lightbox preview with keyboard navigation, and escape-key handling.

nodes.name: PlayerAidManager
nodes.summary: Manages player aid image list with upload (via api.uploadPlayerAid), delete confirmation, and an in-page lightbox with arrow-key and escape navigation.

nodes.name: RulesUrlEditor.tsx
nodes.summary: Inline editor component for a game's rules URL that validates against a Google Drive URL pattern and persists changes via api.updateRulesUrl.

nodes.name: RulesUrlEditor
nodes.summary: Inline edit/save/cancel form for a rules URL with Google Drive regex validation and save-in-progress state management.

nodes.name: TagList.tsx
nodes.summary: Simple presentational component that renders a labeled list of tag badges with support for two visual variants.

nodes.name: AuthContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context and provider for authentication state, implementing optimistic loading, background ping validation, login/logout actions, and automatic redirect on token refresh failure.

nodes.name: AuthProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider that initializes auth state optimistically from stored tokens, validates via api.ping on mount, and wires up the global 401/refresh-failure handler to redirect to /login.

nodes.name: useAuth.ts
nodes.summary: Thin convenience hook that reads from AuthContext and throws if used outside an AuthProvider.

nodes.name: useCollections.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hook exposing collections list query plus create, update, and delete mutations, each invalidating the collections cache on success.

nodes.name: useCollections
nodes.summary: Returns collections list data and three mutation helpers (createCollection, updateCollection, deleteCollection) that automatically invalidate the query cache on success.

nodes.name: useDebounce.ts
nodes.summary: Generic debounce hook that delays propagating a value change by a configurable number of milliseconds using setTimeout/clearTimeout.

nodes.name: useGame.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hook for the game detail page, fetching a single game plus available collections and exposing setCollections and deleteGame mutations with cache invalidation and navigation.

nodes.name: useGame
nodes.summary: Fetches game detail and collections list in parallel; provides setCollections mutation (invalidates game cache) and deleteGame mutation (navigates back to collection on success).

nodes.name: useGames.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hook for the game collection list that debounces the search filter by 300ms before sending a paginated query to the API.

nodes.name: useGames
nodes.summary: Accepts a FilterState, debounces the search term, and queries api.listGames with all active filters; returns games, total count, categories, loading, and error state.

nodes.name: errors.go
nodes.summary: Defines sentinel errors and machine-readable error codes shared across all mbgc services, with Is* helper functions for type-safe error checking via errors.Is.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Go sentinel error pattern with errors.Is wrapping — callers wrap with fmt.Errorf("%w") and check with apierr.Is* helpers.

nodes.name: errors_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the apierr sentinel error helpers, verifying Is* functions detect both direct sentinel matches and wrapped errors.

nodes.name: envelope.go
nodes.summary: Provides standard JSON response wrappers (Response, ListResponse, ErrorResponse) and constructor functions used by all mbgc HTTP handlers to produce consistent wire format.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Go generics (Response[T], ListResponse[T]) for type-safe JSON wrapping; nil-slice coercion in NewList ensures [] not null in JSON output.

nodes.name: envelope_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for envelope constructors verifying nil coercion, pagination metadata fields, and error envelope structure.

nodes.name: middleware.go
nodes.summary: Implements core HTTP middleware for mbgc services including Chain composition, CORS, SecurityHeaders, Recover (panic handler), RequestID injection, and structured request logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Chain wraps handlers in reverse order so first middleware is outermost; statusWriter embeds ResponseWriter to capture status codes without copying the full interface.

nodes.name: middleware_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for HTTP middleware (CORS, SecurityHeaders, Recover, RequestID, Chain, Logger) verifying correct header injection, panic recovery, and structured log output.

nodes.name: ratelimit.go
nodes.summary: Per-IP token-bucket rate limiting middleware using golang.org/x/time/rate, trusting only the rightmost X-Forwarded-For entry to prevent client IP spoofing.
nodes.languageNotes: Background goroutine prunes stale visitor entries every 5 minutes to prevent unbounded map growth; uses rightmost XFF for GCP Cloud Run edge trust model.

nodes.name: ratelimit_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for rate limiting middleware verifying 429 response, slog event emission, and XFF-based client IP extraction.

nodes.name: user.go
nodes.summary: Context accessors for gateway-injected user identity (userID, username, isAdmin) and request ID, enabling type-safe retrieval of JWT-derived claims downstream in handler chains.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses typed contextKey int constants to avoid context key collisions across packages.

nodes.name: user_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for context accessor functions verifying correct retrieval and zero-value behavior of userID, username, isAdmin, and requestID fields.

nodes.name: client.go
nodes.summary: Exports DefaultClient, a shared http.Client with a 10-second timeout, to prevent goroutine exhaustion from hung upstream calls (Supabase Auth, BGG API).

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Go module definition for github.com/LuisMedinaG/mbgc/pkg/shared, declaring the module path and Go version for the shared library used across the monorepo.

nodes.name: go.sum
nodes.summary: Go module checksum lockfile for pkg/shared, recording cryptographic hashes of all direct and transitive dependencies to ensure reproducible builds.

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Go module definition for github.com/LuisMedinaG/mbgc/services/api, declaring dependencies including chi router, golang-migrate, keyfunc for JWKS, slog, and a local replace directive for pkg/shared.

nodes.name: go.sum
nodes.summary: Go module checksum lockfile for services/api, recording cryptographic hashes for all transitive dependencies to ensure reproducible builds.

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Root Go workspace module definition declaring the `github.com/LuisMedinaG/mbgc` module path and requiring Go 1.25; acts as the workspace-level module anchor for the go.work setup.

nodes.name: go.work
nodes.summary: Go workspace file linking the monorepo modules (pkg/shared and services/api) so that local changes to pkg/shared are immediately visible without version bumps.
nodes.languageNotes: go.work workspace enables replace directives across modules without editing go.mod files.

nodes.name: IsUnauthorized
nodes.summary: Checks whether an error wraps either ErrUnauthorized or ErrWrongPassword, treating both as 401 auth failures in HTTP responses.

nodes.name: Chain
nodes.summary: Composes multiple middleware functions onto an HTTP handler, applying them in declaration order (first middleware is outermost in the call stack).

nodes.name: CORS

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the front door to mbgc — a personal board game collection app built as a Go + React monorepo. It sketches the full architecture (Go API, React SPA, Supabase auth, GCP Cloud Run, Cloudflare Pages), lists the quick-start commands, and maps out the CI/CD pipeline. Reading it first answers "What does this project do and how is it organized?" before diving into any code.

tour.title: Monorepo Workspace
tour.description: go.work is the glue that makes the Go side of the monorepo work. It links pkg/shared and services/api into a single Go workspace, meaning local edits to the shared library are instantly visible to the API without publishing a new module version. This file is small but load-bearing: without it, go build would chase a remote module that doesn't exist.
tour.languageLesson: Go workspaces (go.work) were introduced in Go 1.18 to solve multi-module monorepo development. The 'use .' or 'use ./path' directive tells the toolchain to resolve that module from disk rather than the module proxy, enabling simultaneous development across modules without version-bump churn.

tour.title: Shared Library Foundation
tour.description: pkg/shared is the bedrock every Go handler builds on. It provides three packages: apierr defines typed error sentinels (ErrNotFound, ErrUnauthorized, etc.) that map to HTTP status codes, httpx/envelope enforces a consistent JSON response wrapper for all success and error payloads, and httpx/middleware bundles rate limiting, body-size caps, and user-identity extraction. Because this module has fanIn=9 for errors.go alone, understanding it first makes every handler file readable.
tour.languageLesson: Go sentinel errors combined with errors.Is/errors.As let packages declare typed error values that callers can match without importing internal types. pkg/shared/apierr uses this pattern so any handler can call apierr.IsNotFound(err) and get a 404 without touching the database package.

tour.title: API Server Entry Point
tour.description: cmd/server/main.go is the highest fan-out file in the entire codebase (fanOut=35) and the second-highest fan-in (fanIn=12). It runs database migrations at startup via golang-migrate with SQL embedded in the binary, opens a pgxpool connection, initializes JWKS-based JWT verification, seeds the admin user, registers all domain handlers under the chi router, applies the middleware chain (CORS, rate limiting, body limits), and listens for SIGTERM/SIGINT for graceful shutdown. Every architectural decision in the project passes through this file.
tour.languageLesson: Go's embed.FS (used here for SQL migrations) bundles files into the binary at compile time. This means the production Docker image is self-contained — no migration files need to be mounted or downloaded at runtime, which is critical for stateless Cloud Run containers.

tour.title: Server Configuration & Migrations
tour.description: config.go centralizes all environment-variable parsing behind a single Config struct, failing fast at startup if required vars like SUPABASE_URL or DATABASE_URL are absent. embed.go is the companion: it uses //go:embed to load every SQL migration file from the migrations/ directory into the binary, which main.go then hands to golang-migrate. Together these two files enforce the contract that the server is fully self-configuring and self-migrating — no external tooling needed at deploy time.

tour.title: JWT Authentication
tour.description: jwt/verifier.go implements the authentication layer: it fetches Supabase's JWKS endpoint at boot and auto-refreshes the key set, then validates every inbound token's signature, expiry, issuer, and audience. This is the sole enforcement point for multi-tenancy — every handler downstream calls httpx.UserIDFromContext to get the verified user ID without re-validating the token. Understanding this file explains why no handler ever calls Supabase directly for auth.
tour.languageLesson: JWKS (JSON Web Key Sets) allow the API to validate JWTs using only the issuer's public key — the private key never leaves Supabase. The github.com/MicahParks/keyfunc library handles key rotation automatically so the API never needs a restart when Supabase rotates signing keys.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The four SQL migration files define the entire data model. Migration 001 creates the profile.users table (BGG username, admin flag) that the JWT identity maps to. Migration 002 adds games, collections, collection_games, and player_aids — the core domain tables for the app's board game features. Migration 003 introduces rate_limits and sync_log for BGG import throttling. Migration 004 adds the bgg_username column. These files are the source of truth for what every Go store and React hook ultimately reads and writes.
tour.languageLesson: SQL migrations should always come in up/down pairs and be idempotent. golang-migrate tracks applied migrations in the schema_migrations table so replaying them on a fresh DB reaches the same state. The migration files here are embedded in the binary (embed.go from Step 5) and run automatically at startup, making Cloud Run deployments safe without a separate migration job.

tour.title: Game Domain: Handler, Service, Store
tour.description: The game package is the central domain of the app and shows the three-layer pattern used throughout the API. game/model.go (fanIn=7) defines the Game, Collection, and PlayerAid types shared across layers. game/handler.go (fanIn=5, fanOut=11) receives HTTP requests, validates inputs, and delegates to the service. game/service.go (fanIn=6) holds business logic — pagination, filter assembly, ownership checks. game/store.go (fanOut=12) executes parameterized SQL queries against pgxpool. This vertical slice teaches the entire architecture of every other domain package.
tour.languageLesson: Go's interface-based dependency injection makes handler tests fast and DB-free. Each package defines a Store interface; the Service accepts the interface, not *pgxStore. In tests, a hand-written mock satisfies the interface, so handler_test.go and service_test.go never spin up Postgres.

tour.title: BGG Importer
tour.description: The importer package is the most complex domain in the API, with the second-highest fan-out service (fanOut=19) and a dedicated BGG HTTP client (fanOut=14). bgg.go fetches and parses the BoardGameGeek XML API, normalising game metadata into the app's types. importer/service.go orchestrates the full sync: checks per-user rate limits against the rate_limits table, calls bgg.go, deduplicates against existing games, and writes to sync_log. importer/handler.go exposes the trigger endpoints and enforces admin-only access for full refreshes.

tour.title: Frontend Entry & Auth Context
tour.description: web/src/main.tsx mounts the React 19 app, wraps it in the QueryClientProvider and AuthContext, and hands routing to App.tsx. AuthContext.tsx (fanIn=4) holds the Supabase session state and exposes a useAuth hook consumed by every protected route and component. App.tsx (fanOut=14) declares all routes and enforces the authenticated guard — unauthenticated users are redirected to LoginPage before any board game data is fetched.
tour.languageLesson: React Context is used here specifically for auth state because it changes infrequently and needs to be available to every component in the tree. TanStack Query handles all server data separately — mixing auth state into the query cache would cause subtle invalidation bugs on logout.

tour.title: API Client & Query Layer
tour.description: web/src/lib/api.ts is the highest fan-in file in the entire graph (fanIn=16) — every hook and page that talks to the backend goes through it. It centralises the fetch wrapper, injects the Supabase access token, handles response envelope unwrapping, and surfaces typed errors. queryKeys.ts (fanIn=6) provides a single source of truth for TanStack Query cache keys, preventing stale-cache bugs from key string mismatches. queryClient.ts configures retry and stale-time defaults that apply to every query in the app.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Query v5 separates server state (fetched, cached, invalidated by queryClient) from local UI state (useState). The pattern here — one api.ts function per endpoint, one useQuery hook per domain, shared queryKeys — means adding a new endpoint is a three-file change with zero risk of cache-key collision.

tour.title: Frontend Hooks & Game Display
tour.description: The hooks directory provides one TanStack Query hook per domain: useGames for the paginated, filterable game list; useGame for a single game's detail; useCollections for the user's named collections; useProfile for the user's BGG username and admin flag. These hooks are the React mirror of the Go handler/service/store trio — they abstract the network layer so pages declare what data they need, not how to fetch it. GameGrid.tsx and GameCard.tsx (the game list UI) are the most visible output of this data flow.

tour.title: Containerization & Infrastructure
tour.description: The Dockerfile uses a two-stage build: the builder stage compiles the Go binary with CGO_ENABLED=0 into a statically linked executable; the runtime stage copies only that binary into a distroless/static-debian12 image, yielding a production image with no shell, no package manager, and minimal attack surface. The prod Terraform module (infra/environments/prod/main.tf) then provisions Cloud Run, Cloudflare Pages, Supabase auth settings, Workload Identity Federation for keyless GitHub Actions deploys, and GCP monitoring alerts — the full cloud footprint declared as code.
tour.languageLesson: Distroless images contain only the application runtime and its dependencies — no shell, no coreutils, no package manager. This reduces the CVE surface dramatically compared to alpine or debian base images. Combined with CGO_ENABLED=0 (pure Go, no C libraries), the binary runs correctly inside distroless/static with zero system dependencies.

tour.title: CI/CD Pipeline
tour.description: pipeline.yml is the primary GitHub Actions workflow and shows how all the pieces ship together. It uses path-based change detection to run only affected jobs, enforces a branch gate (dev → main is the only deploy path), runs Go tests with -race and a 50% coverage floor, and deploys the API to Cloud Run dev on every push to dev. Pushes to main require a manual approval gate before deploying to prod. The companion infra.yml runs terraform plan on PRs and terraform apply on merge, keeping infrastructure code and application code on separate but parallel lifecycles.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions Workload Identity Federation (used in deploy-cloud-run.yml) lets the workflow authenticate to GCP without storing a service account JSON key as a secret. The OIDC token GitHub mints for the run is exchanged for a short-lived GCP access token, so credentials can't leak even if the repo is compromised.
